what_it_does: Records metadata for a channel attachment that links to an image asset in the design-assets channel.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, no body content
standouts: Target URI contains literal spaces and parentheses: 'image (32)'
conventions: Uses ledger-style YAML frontmatter for attachment metadata
Fields include id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added
concerns: Target URI contains unescaped spaces and parentheses, which may cause parsing or resolution issues
No title, alt text, or MIME type is recorded
No indication of which message or entity owns the attachment
notes: This is a minimal channel-attachment ledger entry pointing to a design-assets image icon named 'image (32)'.
questions: What UI element or message is this attachment associated with?
Why does the target filename include spaces and parentheses instead of a normalized slug?
Is the image:// URI scheme resolvable by the consuming system?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches the content: it is a metadata record for an image attachment in a channel.
intention_signals: It appears to register and timestamp a specific image asset attachment in the design-assets channel so it can be referenced or audited later.
novelty: The target path embeds literal spaces and parentheses rather than a sanitized identifier, which is uncommon for machine-resolvable asset URIs.
contribution: Provides a precise, timestamped mapping from a channel attachment identifier to a specific image asset location in the design-assets channel.
summary_for_embedding: Attachment metadata file att-157c1518 in the design-assets channel references the image asset image://design-assets/icons/image (32) and records an added timestamp of 2026-06-22T12:26:25.672818+00:00.
intention_for_embedding: Record a channel attachment that points to a design-assets image icon for later retrieval, linking, or audit.
